attacking_style: Sprint finish specialist who positions intelligently away from traditional leadout trains, choosing opposite side of road for cleaner finishing lanes in chaotic finales.
team_tactics: Operates independently of conventional leadout structures, relying on personal positioning judgment rather than organized team sprint trains to find optimal finishing position.
terrain_preference: Flat sprint stages with chaotic, disorganized finales where positioning intelligence trumps pure power. Thrives when leadout trains fragment or are unavailable.
vulnerability: Likely struggles against organized leadout trains on straightforward sprint stages where team power dominates; Tour debut suggests limited sample size for tactical assessment.
key_patterns: Exploits opposite side positioning when leadout trains dominate primary lanes
Shows tactical maturity beyond experience level in high-pressure sprint scenarios
Capable of photo-finish execution against top-tier sprinters like Philipsen
form_trajectory: positive: Won Tour debut on Champs-Élysées, demonstrating immediate WorldTour sprint competitiveness and tactical intelligence
